methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: World Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Nominal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Photoroom, an AI & Software company at Series B stage founded by Matthieu Rouif and Eliot Andres, is currently valued at $500M post-money on $43M in total raised capital. The relevant comp universe comprises 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (Palantir), all operating in the AI & Software sector at similar or comparable stages.

Two valuation methods triangulate on defensible ranges. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $950M. Peer-median benchmarking across four same-sector, same-stage companies produces a $5.3B implied value, though this cohort exhibits substantial dispersion: the range spans $1.0B (Nominal) to $32.0B (Safe Superintelligence), with Physical Intelligence at $5.6B and World Labs at $5.0B anchoring the midpoint. These two methods generate a triangulated range of $808M (low), $3.1B (median), and $6.1B (high).

The material gap between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects heterogeneity in Series B valuations within frontier AI. The peer-median approach is constrained by the outsized influence of Safe Superintelligence's $32B valuation; the distribution suggests Photoroom's positioning relative to peers determines credibility of the upper bound. Key uncertainties include revenue traction, unit economics, and whether Photoroom's go-to-market aligns more with infrastructure-layer or application-layer comparables. Current $500M valuation sits below the triangulated median, implying limited upside under peer-based methods.
